---
title: "Asterionellopsis"
subtitle: "Club-shaped pennate cells with a swollen foot pole, radiating from a common centre into star-shaped or spiralled colonies."
categories: [Diatoms]
---

<!-- GENERATED by scripts/build_pages.py — do not edit by hand. Edit meta.yml and re-run the script. -->

**Group:** Diatoms  ·  **Optics:** high-mag, low-mag

## Defining characteristics

Cells are clavate (club-shaped): one end is expanded into a heavier "foot" while the other tapers into a long, fine trailing arm. The foot poles gather at a common centre so the colony radiates outward as a star (stellate) or gentle spiral/helix. Key features: markedly unequal cell ends (inflated foot vs thin tail), central attachment, star or helical colony. Asterionellopsis glacialis is the common coastal/surf-zone species.

## Distinguishing from similar classes

| Similar class | How to tell them apart |
|---|---|
| **thalassionema** | Thalassionema forms stars/zigzags too, but its cells are near-uniform bars joined at the corners; Asterionellopsis cells are clavate with a distinct inflated foot at the colony centre. |
| **pennate** | Label Asterionellopsis when the clavate cells and central star are visible; use Pennate only when the genus is unclear. |

## Ideal images

_No images yet — add files to this folder._

## Challenging images

_No images yet — add files to this folder._

## References

- [Asterionellopsis glacialis — UBC Phytoplankton (EOAS)](https://phytoplankton.eoas.ubc.ca/research/phytoplankton/diatoms/pennate/asterionellopsis/a_glacialis.html)
